Agree. Securing the airway is indeed the highest priority in situations where severe bleeding and traumatic injuries suggest significant underlying issues, including the possibility of internal organ damage or compromised breathing mechanisms. Addressing the airway ensures basic physiological stability and allows for safer, more efficient administration of other treatments without compromising the patient’s ability to maintain oxygenation.
